limits: CKM-conjugate falsifier lane reports non-zero strong residuals (max_abs_residual=166) under explicit conjugate-phase transport assumptions; this lane is non-blocking discovery, not promotion-gating, in structure_first scope.
Dynamic CP-isolation is partially closed: deterministic deep-cone weak-leakage gate suites pass, but physically faithful CKM-sector transport coupling tests are still open.
Continuum EFT mapping from discrete Fano sign-balance to the F*F_tilde theta coefficient remains an explicit bridge assumption and is not yet formally closed.
bridge_comment: Bridge assumptions are explicit and scoped correctly for supported_bridge: strong-sector CP isolation under matched-lane weak coupling and discrete-to-continuum correspondence remain open and are not hidden inside implementation details.
falsification_comment: Falsification conditions now cover structural and bridge scenarios; deterministic structural lanes include matched and conjugate CKM transport probes, while bridge falsification remains theory-level.
review_summary: Structural THETA witnesses remain strong: Lean proofs for CP involution, orientation closure, and exact sign-balance are present; deterministic Python traces pass; weak-leakage gate suites (baseline and matched CKM-like) are all-zero across preregistered deep-cone stress grids. A parallel CKM-conjugate falsifier lane now reports non-zero residuals under stronger conjugate-phase transport assumptions. This does not invalidate the current structure_first supported_bridge lane, but it is an explicit limitation requiring further model-faithful weak-sector closure before any stronger claim can be made.
